element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player assigns strength, intelligence, charisma to The Nameless One; establishes Fighter class baseline
Tutorial space teaching isometric navigation, clicking to move, basic interaction with painted scenery
Core narrative hook: player learns The Nameless One is immortal with memory gaps; journal system introduced
Seven potential companions available; max party size 5; conversation system with party dynamics
Many quests resolved through dialogue rather than combat; stealth as alternative to violence
The Nameless One begins 'true neutral'; alignment shifts based on player actions; NPC reactions adjust accordingly
Player can freely change from Fighter to Thief or Wizard; allows build experimentation
Alternative to traditional armor; applies to The Nameless One and certain companions for ability enhancement
Spells accessed via hotkeys, quick slots, or radial menu; supports active spell casting
Story moves to other planes: Baator, Carceri; reveals past incarnations and prior world influence
Multiple factions in Sigil offer allegiances; player can join several; affects story and NPC relations
Slowly revealed throughout; central to understanding why The Nameless One was cursed with immortality

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to Sigil safe havens after planar exploration
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering moral choice moments affecting alignment
Meeting characters connected to past incarnations
Immortality mechanic: death/respawn revealing new narrative threads
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Narrative-driven with episodic tension cycles; exploration punctuated by dialogue encounters; combat optional; memory revelation paced across entire playthrough

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Faction headquarters and companion-specific areas accessible based on player choices and recruitment
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Previous life locations and hidden lore tied to exploration of all accessible planes and Sigil areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle learning curve; The Mortuary tutorial teaches isometric controls and basic interaction; immortality mechanic allows experimentation without permanent failure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complexity increases through faction engagement, alignment consequences, and planar travel introduction; dialogue-heavy encounters require careful choice management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Narrative intensity rises as planar exploration deepens; previous life revelations compound moral weight; combat encounters more dangerous but avoidable through dialogue mastery
notable_spikes: Encounters with characters from past incarnations
Late-game confrontations tied to immortality curse revelation

notable_level_patterns.description: Central city serves as hub with multiple faction-controlled districts and companion recruitment points; acts as safe haven between planar excursions
Distinct planes (Baator, Carceri) accessed via portals; each plane reveals portion of past and introduces new narrative stakes; increased danger outside Sigil
Areas designed around companion backstories and recruitment; conversation-dense; often avoidable combat focus
Many 'levels' or encounters resolve through conversation trees rather than spatial navigation puzzles; alignment choices embedded in dialogue
